executive_summary: Three critical vulnerabilities are actively being exploited in the wild right now: F5 BIG-IP (CVE-2025-53521, upgraded from DoS to full remote code execution), Fortinet FortiClient EMS, and Citrix NetScaler (CVE-2026-3055). The Axios npm supply chain attack is especially severe—a compromised maintainer account pushed malware into a JavaScript library downloaded 400 million times monthly, potentially impacting countless web applications. Iranian state hackers breached FBI Director Kash Patel's personal email and posted his CV and photos online, while Russian APT Star Blizzard has adopted the DarkSword iOS exploit kit. The European Commission confirmed a data breach after the Europa.eu hack, with ShinyHunters claiming involvement.

canada_landscape.summary: No specific Canadian retail or financial institution breaches were identified in this reporting period. Multiple articles reference Canada in phishing and tax scam campaigns targeting Canadian citizens during tax season. The EvilTokens phishing-as-a-service platform was noted as targeting Microsoft accounts with Canada among affected regions.

report_2w.executive_summary: F5 BIG-IP APM systems are under active attack via CVE-2025-53521, a remote code execution flaw now added to CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. Iranian threat actor Handala successfully breached FBI Director Kash Patel's personal Gmail account. Supply chain attacks continue with TeamPCP compromising the popular Telnyx package on PyPI (742,000 downloads), hiding malware in WAV audio files, and exploiting Aqua Security's Trivy scanner (CVE-2026-33634)—both vulnerabilities now added to CISA's KEV. The European Commission confirmed a cyberattack following an AWS account breach, with ShinyHunters claiming theft of 350GB of data. Citrix NetScaler devices face active reconnaissance for CVE-2026-3055, a critical memory overread bug rated CVSS 9.3.

items.description: Remote code execution vulnerability in F5 BIG-IP APM systems

items.description: Vulnerability referenced in Langflow RCE exploitation context

items.name: Handala (Iran-linked)
items.targets: FBI Director Kash Patel's personal Gmail account, Stryker (wiper attack)
items.ttps: T1566 Phishing, T1078 Valid Accounts, T1485 Data Destruction (wiper). Targeted spear-phishing campaign against high-value government targets.

items.name: TA446
items.ttps: T1566.001 Spear-phishing Attachment. Deploying DarkSword iOS exploit kit in targeted spear-phishing campaigns.

items.name: TeamPCP
items.targets: Developers using Telnyx PyPI package (742,000 downloads), Aqua Security Trivy scanner users
items.ttps: T1195.002 Supply Chain Compromise, T1027.003 Steganography (hiding payload in WAV files), T1059 Command and Scripting Interpreter. Campaign entering monetization phase after 48-hour operational pause.

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: European Commission (claims 350GB data breach), BreachForums (340k users' emails and passwords exposed)
items.ttps: T1530 Data from Cloud Storage, T1078 Valid Accounts. Claims breach via AWS account compromise and Salesforce access.

items.name: Bearlyfy
items.ttps: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact. Deploying custom GenieLocker ransomware against Russian organizations.

items.name: Red Menshen (China-linked)
items.ttps: T1014 Rootkit. Deploying stealthy BPFDoor implants in telecommunications infrastructure for espionage.

items.name: Silver Fox
items.ttps: T1566.001 Spear-phishing Attachment, T1204.002 Malicious File. Tax-themed phishing campaigns targeting Japanese organizations.

canada_landscape.summary: No specific Canadian CCCS advisories identified in this reporting period. Multiple articles reference Canada in general geopolitical and trade contexts, but no direct cyber incidents targeting Canadian critical infrastructure, retailers, or financial institutions were reported. One article discusses LEO satellite telecommunications security with Canadian relevance.
